column8:
During the technical interaction, the interviewer asked conceptual and discussion-based questions. One of the questions asked was “Is programming the future?”, followed by questions on basic technical fundamentals.
 
 The discussion covered:
 
 Programming fundamentals
 
 Python basics
 
 SQL queries and database concepts
 
 Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) concepts
 
 Computer Networks basics
 
 Academic projects
 
 Internship experience
 
 The interviewer mainly focused on understanding my fundamentals, clarity of concepts, and learning mindset.

column10:

column11: The online exam consisted of multiple-choice questions on Quantitative Aptitude, Logical Reasoning, Programming MCQs, and basic coding concepts.
column13:
Quantitative Aptitude:
 
 Average speed of a vehicle when speeds are different.
 
 Percentage increase and decrease problems.
 
 Time and work (men–days).
 
 Remainder and divisibility problems.
 
 Probability-based questions (dice).
 
 Number of cubes in a cubical block.
 
 Logical Reasoning:
 
 Number series completion.
 
 Odd one out (numbers and shapes).
 
 Coding–decoding based on alphabet shifting.
 
 Direction sense and syllogism questions.
 
 Analogy-based reasoning questions.
 
 Verbal Ability:
 
 Word analogy questions.
 
 Sentence completion.
 
 Basic grammar and vocabulary-based questions.
